prompt: An ACL has a condition and a script. How are they combined to grant access?
choices: Both the condition and the script must evaluate to true (in addition to any required role)
Only one of them needs to be true
The script is ignored if a condition exists
They are averaged into a numeric score
explanation: An ACL grants access only when the role, condition, and script requirements are all satisfied.

prompt: How does Application Scope help protect artifacts during collaborative development with Delegated Development?
choices: Admins can grant specific developers rights to edit only certain file types within a scoped application
It disables all editing for everyone
It merges all applications into global scope
It removes the need for source control
explanation: Delegated Development, used with scoped apps, lets admins grant developers permission to work on specific application file types while protecting the rest.

prompt: Which security artifacts can restrict access to an entire application module? (Choose two.)
choices: Roles required on the module
Roles required on the application menu
A transform map on the module
An import set row for the module
explanation: Both the application menu and its modules can require roles; users without those roles cannot see or open the module.

prompt: When a scoped application needs to call an API in another scope, what must be configured?
choices: Cross-scope access privileges authorizing the specific runtime access
Nothing; all scopes can call each other freely
A new instance login page
A homepage gauge widget
explanation: Cross-scope access records must authorize a scoped app to access resources in another scope, enforcing the scope protection model.

prompt: Which automation tool provides a low-code, visual way to build multi-step process logic triggered by records or schedules?
choices: Flow Designer
Form Designer for layouts
Access Control rules for records
Dictionary Entries for columns
explanation: Flow Designer offers a low-code interface to build flows with triggers, actions, and subflows for process automation.
